skill.name: nextjs-best-practices
skill.description: Guides Next.js App Router development with practical patterns for Server Components, routing, caching, metadata, and performance.
skill.summary: Apply concise Next.js App Router best practices during code planning and review.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as shell execution and prose keywords as weak cryptography. Review found no executable scripts, no network behavior, no credential access, and no prompt injection attempt in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Detected as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md lines 17, 28, 65, 66, 67, 68, 69, 75, 76, 77, 150, 151, 152, and 187. These locations are Markdown code fences or inline code examples for Next.js file names and routing patterns, not executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain Markdown formatting and static documentation only. No shell, Ruby, or runtime command execution syntax is present outside prose examples.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators at SKILL.md lines 3, 95, and 130. These lines contain a skill description, status code guidance, and metadata description length guidance, not cryptographic functions or algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms appear inside ordinary English documentation. There is no code path that imports, configures, or invokes weak cryptographic algorithms.

content.value_statement: Next.js App Router projects can suffer from unclear component boundaries, weak routing structure, and inefficient data fetching. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a compact checklist for applying practical App Router patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Server Components or Client Components.
Summarizes data fetching, caching, and revalidation choices.
Lists App Router file conventions such as page, layout, loading, error, and not-found files.
Guides API route handler design with validation, status codes, and error handling.
Highlights performance practices for images, bundles, metadata, and loading states.
Provides project structure patterns for route groups, dashboards, APIs, and shared UI.
limitations: It is a documentation-only skill and does not run tests or inspect projects by itself.
It does not replace current Next.js release notes or official migration guides.
It gives general patterns, not application-specific architecture decisions without code context.
It does not include security hardening beyond input validation and error handling reminders.

use_cases.title: Plan a New App Router Feature
use_cases.description: Use the skill to choose component boundaries, route files, and data fetching before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Review a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to spot avoidable client components, missing loading states, and unclear caching behavior.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Guidance
use_cases.description: Use the concise patterns as shared guidance for App Router conventions across a team.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Component Type
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single component idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the nextjs-best-practices skill to decide whether this component should be a Server Component or Client Component. Explain the reason and list any split needed.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Route Files
prompt_templates.scenario: Designing a new page or section.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the nextjs-best-practices skill to outline the App Router files for this feature. Include page, layout, loading, error, and not-found recommendations where useful.

prompt_templates.title: Review Data Fetching
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the nextjs-best-practices skill to review this data fetching plan. Identify whether static caching, revalidation, no-store, or server actions fit each data source.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Feature Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before implementation or merge.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the nextjs-best-practices skill to audit this Next.js App Router feature for component boundaries, routing, caching, metadata, loading states, error states, and bundle impact.

output_examples.input: Review a product details page that fetches catalog data and has an add-to-cart button.
output_examples.output: The catalog view should stay server-rendered, with cached product data and metadata generated per product. The add-to-cart control should be a small Client Component or server action boundary.

output_examples.input: Plan a dashboard route with slow analytics data and user-specific filters.
output: Use a dashboard layout for shared navigation.
Add loading and error states near the analytics route.
Fetch user-specific analytics dynamically and keep filter controls client-side.

output_examples.input: Check whether a marketing page should use client-side fetching.
output_examples.output: Prefer a Server Component with cached fetches for marketing content. Add client components only for interactive elements such as forms, menus, or personalization controls.

best_practices: Start with Server Components and add Client Components only for state, effects, or event handlers.
Define caching behavior deliberately for each data source, including revalidation or no-store when needed.
Include loading, error, and not-found states for routes that depend on remote or dynamic data.
anti_patterns: Adding use client to every component without checking whether interactivity is required.
Fetching dynamic data in Client Components when Server Components or server actions fit better.
Skipping route-level metadata, loading states, or error boundaries for user-facing pages.

faq.question: Does this skill support the Next.js App Router?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance focuses on App Router concepts, including Server Components, route files, caching, and server actions.

faq.question: Can it migrate a Pages Router project automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can guide migration decisions, but it does not perform an automatic migration by itself.

faq.question: Does it execute commands or install packages?
faq.answer: No. The skill is Markdown guidance and only lists Read, Write, Edit, Glob, and Grep as allowed tools.

faq.question: Is this useful for code review?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides concise checks for component boundaries, routing conventions, caching, metadata, and performance.

faq.question: Does it include official Next.js version-specific rules?
faq.answer: No. It gives general App Router practices and should be paired with official documentation for version-specific changes.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
